question: Implement a `StringWrapper` class that manages a dynamically allocated C-style character array (char*). Write a copy constructor that performs a deep copy of the string data, ensuring independent memory management for copied objects.

test_cases.input: StringWrapper s1("Hello"); StringWrapper s2 = s1; s1.modify("World");
test_cases.expected:
s1: String: "World" (Length: 5, ...)
s2: String: "World" (Length: 5, ...)
(Expected s2 to remain "Hello", but it changed due to shallow copy. Also, potential runtime crash due to double-free.)

s3: String: "Test" (Length: 4, ...)
s4: String: "Test" (Length: 4, ...)
(Expected independent copies, but they share memory, leading to double-free on destruction.)
